channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Alibaba Approves Qwen Lead Lin Junyang's Resignation; CTO Zhou Jingren Assumes Control, DeepMind's Zhou Hao Joins
item.description: Alibaba Group CEO Eddie Wu approved the resignation of Qwen lead Lin Junyang on March 5, 2026, following his sudden departure announcement, with DeepMind's Zhou Hao joining to lead post-training efforts as the company restructures its AI team toward horizontal specialization.

item.title: Exoskeleton Robotics Firm RoboCT Raises USD 13.7 Million Series B+ Round
item.description: RoboCT, an exoskeleton robotics firm, raised over RMB 100 million (approx. USD 13.7 million) in Series B+ funding on March 5, 2026, led by ABC Capital, to expand its consumer exoskeleton business, with founder Wang Tian projecting 60,000-100,000 units shipped in 2026.

item.title: Hydrogen Material Specialist Shengshui Tech Secures Over USD 13.7 Million Series A
item.description: Shengshui Tech, a hydrogen materials specialist founded by a USTC team, secured over RMB 100 million (approx. USD 13.7 million) in Series A funding on March 5, 2026, led by NIO Capital, to scale production of membrane electrodes that reduce PEM electrolyzer costs to RMB 1.177 million per megawatt.

item.title: Robot Era Raises USD 137 Million Strategic Round, Valuation Exceeds USD 1.37 Billion
item.description: Robot Era, the only embodied intelligence company with equity ownership by Tsinghua University, raised RMB 1 billion (approx. USD 137 million) in a strategic round on March 5, 2026, pushing its valuation past RMB 10 billion (approx. USD 1.37 billion), with investors including Samsung and Singtel.

item.title: Giga AI Completes Nearly USD 137 Million Pre-B Round
item.description: Giga AI, a company specializing in embodied foundation models, completed a nearly RMB 1 billion (approx. USD 137 million) Pre-B round on March 5, 2026, with backing from SINO IC Capital, CICC Capital, and existing investors, to advance its world model technology and scale production of its Maker...

item.title: Europol-Led Operation Takes Down Tycoon 2FA Phishing-as-a-Service Linked to 64,000 Attacks
item.description: Tycoon 2FA, one of the prominent phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) toolkits that allowed cybercriminals to stage adversary-in-the-middle (AitM) credential harvesting attacks at scale, was dismantled by a coalition of law enforcement agencies and security companies. The subscription-based phishing kit,...

item.title: FBI and Europol Seize LeakBase Forum Used to Trade Stolen Credentials
item.description: A joint law enforcement operation has dismantled LeakBase, one of the world's largest online forums for cybercriminals to buy and sell stolen data and cybercrime tools. The LeakBase forum, per the U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ), had over 142,000 members and more than 215,000 messages between...

item.title: Asymmetric Goal Drift in Coding Agents Under Value Conflict
item.description: arXiv:2603.03456v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agentic coding agents are increasingly deployed autonomously, at scale, and over long-context horizons. Throughout an agent's lifetime, it must navigate tensions between explicit instructions, learned values, and environmental pressures, often in...

item.title: Build, Judge, Optimize: A Blueprint for Continuous Improvement of Multi-Agent Consumer Assistants
item.description: arXiv:2603.03565v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Conversational shopping assistants (CSAs) represent a compelling application of agentic AI, but moving from prototype to production reveals two underexplored challenges: how to evaluate multi-turn interactions and how to optimize tightly coupled...

item.title: Mozi: Governed Autonomy for Drug Discovery LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.03655v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Tool-augmented large language model (LLM) agents promise to unify scientific reasoning with computation, yet their deployment in high-stakes domains like drug discovery is bottlenecked by two critical barriers: unconstrained tool-use governance and...

item.title: MAGE: Meta-Reinforcement Learning for Language Agents toward Strategic Exploration and Exploitation
item.description: arXiv:2603.03680v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) agents have demonstrated remarkable proficiency in learned tasks, yet they often struggle to adapt to non-stationary environments with feedback. While In-Context Learning and external memory offer some flexibility, they fail...

item.title: AI4S-SDS: A Neuro-Symbolic Solvent Design System via Sparse MCTS and Differentiable Physics Alignment
item.description: arXiv:2603.03686v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Automated design of chemical formulations is a cornerstone of materials science, yet it requires navigating a high-dimensional combinatorial space involving discrete compositional choices and continuous geometric constraints. Existing Large Language...

item.title: Knowledge Graph and Hypergraph Transformers with Repository-Attention and Journey-Based Role Transport
item.description: arXiv:2603.03304v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present a concise architecture for joint training on sentences and structured data while keeping knowledge and language representations separable. The model treats knowledge graphs and hypergraphs as structured instances with role slots and encodes...

item.title: AOI: Turning Failed Trajectories into Training Signals for Autonomous Cloud Diagnosis
item.description: arXiv:2603.03378v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents offer a promising data-driven approach to automating Site Reliability Engineering (SRE), yet their enterprise deployment is constrained by three challenges: restricted access to proprietary data, unsafe action...

item.title: RADAR: Learning to Route with Asymmetry-aware DistAnce Representations
item.description: arXiv:2603.03388v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent neural solvers have achieved strong performance on vehicle routing problems (VRPs), yet they mainly assume symmetric Euclidean distances, restricting applicability to real-world scenarios. A core challenge is encoding the relational features in...

item.title: Towards Improved Sentence Representations using Token Graphs
item.description: arXiv:2603.03389v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Obtaining a single-vector representation from a Large Language Model's (LLM) token-level outputs is a critical step for nearly all sentence-level tasks. However, standard pooling methods like mean or max aggregation treat tokens as an independent set,...

item.title: Heterogeneous Time Constants Improve Stability in Equilibrium Propagation
item.description: arXiv:2603.03402v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Equilibrium propagation (EP) is a biologically plausible alternative to backpropagation for training neural networks. However, existing EP models use a uniform scalar time step dt, which corresponds biologically to a membrane time constant that is...

item.title: AriadneMem: Threading the Maze of Lifelong Memory for LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.03290v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Long-horizon LLM agents require memory systems that remain accurate under fixed context budgets. However, existing systems struggle with two persistent challenges in long-term dialogue: (i) \textbf{disconnected evidence}, where multi-hop answers...

item.title: One Bias After Another: Mechanistic Reward Shaping and Persistent Biases in Language Reward Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.03291v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reward Models (RMs) are crucial for online alignment of language models (LMs) with human preferences. However, RM-based preference-tuning is vulnerable to reward hacking, whereby LM policies learn undesirable behaviors from flawed RMs. By...

item.title: From Conflict to Consensus: Boosting Medical Reasoning via Multi-Round Agentic RAG
item.description: arXiv:2603.03292v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) exhibit high reasoning capacity in medical question-answering, but their tendency to produce hallucinations and outdated knowledge poses critical risks in healthcare fields. While Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)...

item.title: SE-Search: Self-Evolving Search Agent via Memory and Dense Reward
item.description: arXiv:2603.03293v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Retrieval augmented generation (RAG) reduces hallucinations and factual errors in large language models (LLMs) by conditioning generation on retrieved external knowledge. Recent search agents further cast RAG as an autonomous, multi-turn...

item.title: Fine-Tuning and Evaluating Conversational AI for Agricultural Advisory
item.description: arXiv:2603.03294v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models show promise for agricultural advisory, yet vanilla models exhibit unsupported recommendations, generic advice lacking specific, actionable detail, and communication styles misaligned with smallholder farmer needs. In high stakes...

item.title: Nation-State iOS Exploit Kit ‘Coruna’ Found Powering Global Attacks
item.description: Google and iVerify analysis reveals a powerful exploit kit originally used by Russian state actors that is now appearing in broader criminal campaigns. The post Nation-State iOS Exploit Kit ‘Coruna’ Found Powering Global Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: [AINews] Is Harness Engineering real?
item.description: a quiet day lets us reflect on a central debate in AI engineering

item.title: User may experience errors in ChatGPT
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied the mitigation and are monitoring the recovery.Affected components Conversations (Degraded performance)

item.title: Disruption with some GitHub services
item.description: Mar 5, 01:30 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available.Mar 5, 01:30 UTCUpdate - Copilot coding agent mission control is fully restored. Tasks are...

item.title: Elevated Durable Objects and R2 API Latency
item.description: Mar 5, 01:30 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 5, 00:34 UTCInvestigating - We are investigating elevated latency affecting Durable Objects and R2 API requests in Western North America region. Some requests may experience timeouts or increase response times.

item.title: Some OpenAI models degraded in Copilot
item.description: Mar 5, 01:13 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available.Mar 5, 01:13 UTCUpdate - The issues with our upstream model provider have been resolved, and...

item.title: API Error Rates
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Fine-tuning (Operational) Files (Operational) Moderations (Operational) Codex (Operational) Audio (Operational) Realtime (Operational) Embeddings (Operational) Responses (Operational) Images (Operational) Batch...

item.title: Every Agent Needs a Box — Aaron Levie, Box
item.description: The reception to our recent post on Code Reviews has been strong. Catch up!

item.title: Cloudflare Dashboard displaying incorrect API token type
item.description: Mar 4, 18:45 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 4, 18:17 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Mar 4, 18:13 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating an issue with the Cloudflare Dashboard where Account API Tokens are not being displayed...

item.title: Tycoon 2FA Phishing Platform Dismantled in Global Takedown
item.description: The phishing-as-a-service platform was used to send fraudulent emails to over 500,000 organizations every month. The post Tycoon 2FA Phishing Platform Dismantled in Global Takedown appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: RoboCasa365: A Large-Scale Simulation Framework for Training and Benchmarking Generalist Robots
item.description: Recent advances in robot learning have accelerated progress toward generalist robots that can perform everyday tasks in human environments. Yet it remains difficult to gauge how close we are to this vision. The field lacks a reproducible, large-scale benchmark for systematic evaluation. To fill...

item.title: New LexisNexis Data Breach Confirmed After Hackers Leak Files
item.description: The hackers claim to have stolen 2GB of files, including 400,000 personal information records. The post New LexisNexis Data Breach Confirmed After Hackers Leak Files appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: 149 Hacktivist DDoS Attacks Hit 110 Organizations in 16 Countries After Middle East Conflict
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of a surge in retaliatory hacktivist activity following the U.S.-Israel coordinated military campaign against Iran, codenamed Epic Fury and Roaring Lion. "The hacktivist threat in the Middle East is highly lopsided, with two groups, Keymous+ and DieNet, driving...

item.title: CNI Degraded Performance
item.description: Mar 4, 16:30 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is currently investigating issues related to CNI. Impacted customers may see connectivity issues through Newark, NJ (EWR) data center. Customers using Equinix Cloud Connect in EWR may experience intermittent connectivity or loss of service. We are working...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Haiku 4.5
item.description: Mar 4, 16:13 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 4, 15:58 UTCInvestigating - The earlier issues with Haiku 4.5 have reappeared.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.6
item.description: Mar 4, 15:57 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 4, 14:14 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Zurich Acquires Beazley in $11 Billion Deal to Lead Cyberinsurance
item.description: The deal awaits final shareholder and regulatory approvals and is expected to be completed in the second half of 2026. The post Zurich Acquires Beazley in $11 Billion Deal to Lead Cyberinsurance appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Degradation-based augmented training for robust individual animal re-identification
item.description: Wildlife re-identification aims to recognise individual animals by matching query images to a database of previously identified individuals, based on their fine-scale unique morphological characteristics. Current state-of-the-art models for multispecies re- identification are based on deep metric...

item.title: Hacker Conversations: Inti De Ceukelaire, Raging Against the Machine Creatively
item.description: A Belgian national, De Ceukelaire’ did not set out to be a hacker. Like many hackers he was born with the potential to become one and only gradually realized he is one. The post Hacker Conversations: Inti De Ceukelaire, Raging Against the Machine Creatively appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Haiku 4.5
item.description: Mar 4, 14:55 UTCResolved - The incident for Haiku 4.5 has been between 6:45 PT / 14:45 UTC and 6:50 PT / 14:50 UTC.Mar 4, 14:47 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: EgoPoseFormer v2: Accurate Egocentric Human Motion Estimation for AR/VR
item.description: Egocentric human motion estimation is essential for AR/VR experiences, yet remains challenging due to limited body coverage from the egocentric viewpoint, frequent occlusions, and scarce labeled data. We present EgoPoseFormer v2, a method that addresses these challenges through two key...

item.title: Bridging the operational AI gap
item.description: The transformational potential of AI is already well established. Enterprise use cases are building momentum and organizations are transitioning from pilot projects to AI in production. Companies are no longer just talking about AI; they are redirecting budgets and resources to make it happen. Many...

item.title: Coruna iOS Exploit Kit Uses 23 Exploits Across Five Chains Targeting iOS 13–17.2.1
item.description: Google said it identified a "new and powerful" exploit kit dubbed Coruna (aka CryptoWaters) targeting Apple iPhone models running iOS versions between 13.0 and 17.2.1. The exploit kit featured five full iOS exploit chains and a total of 23 exploits, Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) said....

item.title: The Download: Earth’s rumblings, and AI for strikes on Iran
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Listen to Earth’s rumbling, secret soundtrack The boom of a calving glacier. The crackling rumble of a wildfire. The roar of a surging storm front....

item.title: Domain Registration unavailable
item.description: Mar 4, 12:14 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 4, 10:56 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Mar 4, 09:38 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is aware of, and investigating an issue which potentially impacts multiple customers for domain...

item.title: Cloudflare GraphQL API outage for DNS metrics
item.description: Mar 4, 12:00 UTCResolved - Cloudflare GraphQL API experienced an outage between 11:56 and 17:00 UTC affecting DNS metrics queries across all regions. This issue has been resolved.

item.title: New RFP Template for AI Usage Control and AI Governance
item.description: As AI becomes the central engine for enterprise productivity, security leaders are finally getting the green light — and the budget — to secure it. But there’s a quiet crisis unfolding in the boardroom: many organizations know they need "AI Governance," but they have no idea what they are actually...

item.title: Architecture and evaluation protocol for transformer-based visual object tracking in UAV applications
item.description: Object tracking from Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) is challenged by platform dynamics, camera motion, and limited onboard resources. Existing visual trackers either lack robustness in complex scenarios or are too computationally demanding for real-time embedded use. We propose an Modular...
